notes: Time Rich 08 Ltd v Dbe (HK) Ltd, [2018] HKCA. In its writ the plaintiff claimed HK$4,965,030.10 together with mesne profits, damages, interest and costs for rent-related arrears. Chu J gave summary judgment for HK$2,329,459.59, and the Court of Appeal dismissed the defendants' appeal. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/time-rich-08-ltd-862552192

Castle Global Ltd v Ip Tai Hoi Paul, judgment discussed in [2021] HKCA. The plaintiff had paid deposits totaling HK$5.2 million and claimed a refund of that deposit plus liquidated damages of the same amount under clause 16(b), for an effective monetary claim of HK$10.4 million. The Recorder ordered refund of the deposit and an equivalent sum as agreed compensation. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/castle-global-ltd-v-877756230

S.C. Chow & Associates Ltd v Chow Kit Ming t/a Modern World Printing Design Co, HCA4504/1995, judgment dated 19 February 1997. For Action 4504/95 the plaintiff quantified rent, management fees, rates, electricity charges, and water charges totaling HK$231,533.13, and the court held the plaintiff was entitled to damages in the sum of HK$231,533.13 as claimed. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/s-c-chow-associates-862488473

Lau Tsz Ha v Chui Sang Choy and Another, HCPI 489/2006, decision dated 10 June 2010. The costs decision records that the plaintiff's initial statement of damages in 2006 sought about HK$3 million and the revised version in 2007 sought about HK$6.7 million; it further records that the plaintiff was eventually awarded damages of HK$347,496.10. `initial_claim` is normalized from the revised claim figure described as 'about $6.7 million'. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/lau-tsz-v-chui-862784962

Lo Wai Lin v FWD Life Insurance Company (Bermuda) Ltd, Small Claims Tribunal Appeal No. 6 of 2016. The claimant's original insurance claim totalled HK$35,361, but shortly before trial she withdrew the doctor and eye-centre fee components, leaving a live claim of HK$32,761 based on the hospital discharge statement. The adjudicator first awarded HK$31,991, and on review varied that amount to HK$31,964. This row normalizes `initial_claim` to the live amount pursued after withdrawal and `final_award` to the reviewed amount described in the CFI leave-to-appeal decision. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/wai-lin-v-fwd-862537773

Chu Hon Chong v Wong Kwan Kit and Another, HCPI 784/2008, decision dated 15 January 2010 on scale of costs. The plaintiff's Revised Statement of Damages claimed HK$2,944,056.00. Before quantum trial, the proceedings were concluded on 14 November 2009 when the plaintiff accepted a sanctioned payment for HK$650,000. This row uses the revised pleaded claim and the accepted sanctioned payment as the monetary resolution recorded by the court. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/chu-hon-chong-v-862800830

Yu Wai Kan v Law Cho Tai, HCPI 237/2009, decision dated 27 April 2011 on scale of costs. The plaintiff's Revised Statement of Damages claimed HK$2,705,070.00. The action was settled and concluded on 14 December 2010 with the defendant agreeing to pay HK$850,000 inclusive of interest in full and final settlement. This row uses the revised pleaded claim and the settlement sum expressly recorded in the later court decision. Source: https://vlex.hk/vid/yu-wai-kan-v-862789961

Quartet Music v. Kissimmee Broadcasting, Inc., 795 F. Supp. 1100 (M.D. Fla. 1992). Plaintiffs sought statutory damages of $5,000 for each of 15 infringements, totaling $75,000. The court found $1,500 per infringement appropriate and entered judgment for $22,500 against Augustine Cawley. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/FSupp/795/1100/2596347/

North American Van Lines, Inc. v. North America Moving & Storage, Inc., No. 1:18-cv-00196, Document 51 (N.D. Ind. Feb. 10, 2020). The amended complaint sought $2,000,000 in statutory damages under 15 U.S.C. § 1117(c)(2), but the court held the facts supported cybersquatting relief under 15 U.S.C. § 1117(d) and awarded $100,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/indiana/inndce/1%3A2018cv00196/95002/51/

Magna-RX, Inc. v. Slepicoff, No. 2:05-cv-03545, Document 494 (D. Ariz. 2008). Plaintiff requested $25,000 for trademark infringement plus $25,000 for copyright infringement against each default defendant, totaling $50,000. The court awarded the statutory minimums: $500 for trademark and $750 for copyright, totaling $1,250 per default defendant. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/arizona/azdce/2%3A2005cv03545/289100/494/

Farrington v. Fingerlakes1.com, Inc., No. 6:19-cv-06802, Document 15 (W.D.N.Y. Dec. 15, 2020). Plaintiff sought $5,000 for copyright infringement and $5,000 for the DMCA claim. The court awarded $2,500 for copyright infringement and $5,000 for the DMCA violation, totaling $7,500. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-york/nywdce/6%3A2019cv06802/126524/15/

AllStar Marketing Group, LLC v. 123 Beads Store et al., No. 1:19-cv-03184, Document 55 (S.D.N.Y. 2020). Plaintiff sought $50,000 in statutory damages against each defaulting defendant for willful counterfeiting and related infringement. The court found that request reasonable and approved $50,000 per defaulting defendant. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-york/nysdce/1%3A2019cv03184/513461/55/

J & J Sports Productions, Inc. v. Ochoa, No. 4:17-cv-00245, Document 9 (D. Idaho Sept. 15, 2017). Plaintiff sought $10,000 in statutory damages and $50,000 in enhanced damages under 47 U.S.C. § 605; the court awarded the full requested amount and ordered entry of judgment for $60,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/idaho/iddce/4%3A2017cv00245/39266/9/

Fallaci v. New Gazette Literary Corp., 568 F. Supp. 1172 (S.D.N.Y. 1983). Plaintiff sought $50,000 in statutory damages for willful copyright infringement. The magistrate recommended, and the court adopted, a $10,000 damages award based on twice the estimated $5,000 market value of the republication rights. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/FSupp/568/1172/2374793/

Sky Vapors, LLC v. Blazynski, No. 1:14-cv-01078, Document 27 (W.D.N.Y. Dec. 19, 2018). Plaintiff sought $50,000 in Lanham Act statutory damages and $50,000 in ACPA statutory damages, totaling $100,000. The court awarded $40,000 for trademark infringement and $500 for cybersquatting, totaling $40,500. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-york/nywdce/1:2014cv01078/101190/27/

Joe Hand Promotions, Inc. v. Alez Caribbean Restaurant and Lounge LLC, No. 8:19-cv-00651, Document 25 (M.D. Fla. Sept. 26, 2019). Plaintiff sought $5,000 in statutory damages and $15,000 in additional damages under 47 U.S.C. § 605 for willful signal piracy. The court awarded the requested $20,000 total. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/florida/flmdce/8%3A2019cv00651/361459/25/

Joe Hand Promotions, Inc. v. Garcia-Nunez, No. 6:18-cv-01452, Document 15 (D. Or. June 11, 2019). Plaintiff sought $10,000 in statutory damages and $25,000 in enhanced statutory damages under 47 U.S.C. § 605. The court awarded $3,000 in statutory damages and $10,000 in enhanced statutory damages, for a total of $13,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/oregon/ordce/6%3A2018cv01452/139157/15/

J & J Sports Productions, Inc. v. Enciso-Chavez, No. 6:17-cv-01430, Document 20 (D. Or. Aug. 6, 2018). Plaintiff sought $10,000 in statutory damages, $30,000 in enhanced statutory damages, and $3,000 for trespass to chattels. The court awarded $3,000 in statutory damages and $15,000 in enhanced statutory damages, while denying the $3,000 trespass request; total monetary award on damages was $18,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/oregon/ordce/6%3A2017cv01430/133186/20/

G&G Closed Circuit Events LLC v. Alcantara, No. 2:18-cv-02836, Document 40 (D. Ariz. Apr. 30, 2020). Plaintiff requested $10,000 in statutory damages and $30,000 in enhanced statutory damages under 47 U.S.C. § 605. The court found the requested statutory damages of $10,000 and a reduced enhanced component of $20,000 reasonable, for a total of $30,000 against the defaulting defendant. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/arizona/azdce/2%3A2018cv02836/1122042/40/

Stokes v. Gold Productions Inc, No. 5:22-cv-00538, Document 13 (W.D. Okla. Jan. 10, 2023). Plaintiff sought statutory damages of $30,000 for each of four infringed photographs, totaling $120,000. The court instead awarded $5,000 per photograph, for a total statutory award of $20,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/oklahoma/okwdce/5%3A2022cv00538/118438/13/

Wareka v. Dolce Vita Health Center, Inc., No. 2:24-cv-01440, Document 27 (E.D. Cal. Oct. 17, 2025). Plaintiff requested total statutory damages of $24,400 across one copyright claim and two CMI claims. The court recommended $1,500 for copyright infringement plus $500 and $500 for the two CMI claims, totaling $2,500. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2024cv01440/446904/27/

CN Investors LLC v. CN Investors LLC, No. 2:25-cv-00533, Document 18 (D. Nev. May 5, 2026). Plaintiff sought the maximum ACPA statutory damages of $100,000 for each of two cybersquatting domains, totaling $200,000. The court found that maximum relief unwarranted and awarded $25,000 per violation, for a total of $50,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/nevada/nvdce/2%3A2025cv00533/173773/18/

Amazon.com, Inc. et al. v. Pionera, Inc., No. 2:22-cv-01491, Document 31 (E.D. Cal. July 7, 2023). Plaintiffs sought $500,000 for each of two trademark infringements plus $100,000 for one cybersquatting violation, totaling $1,100,000. The court recommended $200,000 per mark and $25,000 for cybersquatting, for a total statutory award of $425,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2022cv01491/415288/31/

Chanel, Inc. v. Fe Corp., No. 2:23-cv-02946, Document 20 (E.D. Cal. Aug. 6, 2024). Plaintiff moved for default judgment seeking $150,000 in Lanham Act statutory damages. River Light V, L.P. et al. v. Zhangyali, No. 1:15-cv-05918, Document 80 (N.D. Ill. Aug. 29, 2016). Plaintiffs sought $150,000 in Lanham Act statutory damages for counterfeit Tory Burch goods. Martinka v. Hagedorn Communications, Inc., No. 1:24-cv-03601, Document 23 (S.D.N.Y. Sept. 15, 2025). Plaintiff sought $3,650.00 in statutory damages for willful copyright infringement. The court found a reduced award appropriate and awarded $1,500.00 in statutory damages. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/new-york/nysdce/1%3A2024cv03601/621196/23/

Lynk Media LLC v. Taylor, No. 2:24-cv-02706, Document 35 (E.D. Cal. Feb. 10, 2026). Plaintiff sought statutory damages in the total amount of $12,500.00, calculated from a $2,500 licensing fee multiplied by five for willful infringement. The court recommended awarding the requested $12,500.00 in statutory damages. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2024cv02706/453626/35/

G & G Closed Circuit Events, LLC v. Port City Sports Bar and Grill, LLC, No. 2:22-cv-01059, Document 34 (E.D. Cal. Sept. 6, 2023). Plaintiff sought $5,200 in statutory damages and $25,000 in enhanced damages under 47 U.S.C. 搂 605, for a total statutory request of $30,200, plus $1,300 in conversion damages. The court recommended $2,600 in statutory damages and $3,000 in enhanced damages, for a total statutory award of $5,600. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2022cv01059/412514/34/

J & J Sports Productions, Inc. v. Arteaga, No. 1:11-cv-01980, Document 13 (E.D. Cal. May 11, 2012). Plaintiff requested the maximum $10,000 in statutory damages and $100,000 in enhanced statutory damages under 47 U.S.C. 搂 605, totaling $110,000, plus $1,000 for conversion. The court recommended $10,000 in statutory damages and $5,000 in enhanced damages, for a total statutory award of $15,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/1%3A2011cv01980/232292/13/

Joe Hand Promotions, Inc. v. Santoyo, No. 1:12-cv-01884, Document 14 (E.D. Cal. Apr. 5, 2013). Plaintiff requested $110,000 in statutory and enhanced damages under 47 U.S.C. 搂 605 plus $1,100 in conversion damages, for a total request of $111,100. The court rejected the overreaching request and recommended only $1,400 in statutory damages, while separately allowing $1,100 on conversion. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/1%3A2012cv01884/247061/14/

Stross v. Smith Rock Masonry Company LLC, No. 6:19-cv-01394, Document 26 (D. Or. June 16, 2021). Plaintiff sought $10,000 in statutory damages for copyright infringement. The court found defendant's infringement non-willful and awarded $1,500 in statutory damages instead. Broadcast Music, Inc. v. Larkin, 672 F. Supp. 531 (D. Me. Oct. 20, 1987). Plaintiff sought statutory damages of $1,500 per infringing act for nine unauthorized public performances, totaling $13,500. The court found the request reasonable and awarded $1,500 for each of the nine infringements, for a total statutory award of $13,500. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/FSupp/672/531/2091461/

Bravado International Group Merchandising Services, Inc. v. Cha, No. 2:09-cv-09066, Document 203 (C.D. Cal. Feb. 11, 2011). Plaintiff requested $328,500.00 in statutory damages tied to infringement of Michael Jackson copyrights and related publicity-rights claims. The court held that request excessive and instead awarded $40,000 under 17 U.S.C. 搂 504 and $750 under California Civil Code 搂 3344.1, for a total statutory award of $40,750. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/cacdce/2%3A2009cv09066/460771/203/

Natural Hair Growth Centers of Arizona, LLC v. Edmund, No. 3:19-cv-00026, Document 13 (S.D. Cal. May 23, 2019). Plaintiff requested total statutory damages of $1,040,000: $15,000 per image for 46 images, $150,000 each for two willful copyright violations, and $25,000 for each of two DMCA violations. The court substantially reduced the request and awarded $45,000 total statutory damages. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/casdce/3%3A2019cv00026/609705/13/

Three Lakes Design v. Savala, No. 2:17-cv-01757, Document 31 (E.D. Cal. Jan. 29, 2019). Plaintiff sought $292,100 in statutory damages for unauthorized use of copyrighted photographs. The court found that amount excessive and instead awarded $5,000 per infringed work across 23 photographs, for a total statutory award of $115,000. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/california/caedce/2%3A2017cv01757/321130/31/

GS Holistic LLC v. Thana LLC, No. 2:23-cv-00376, Document 30 (W.D. Wash. Apr. 18, 2024). Plaintiff sought $150,000 in statutory damages, calculated as $50,000 for each of three registered Stundenglass trademarks. Source: https://law.justia.com/cases/federal/district-courts/washington/wawdce/2%3A2023cv00376/319878/30/

Amazon.com Inc. v. Wong, No. 2:19-cv-00990, Document 130 (W.D. Wash. July 3, 2024). For the Nite Ize trademark claims, plaintiffs requested $865,237 in statutory damages against Mr. Wong, based on three times $288,424.60 in counterfeit-product sales through his selling accounts. The court reduced the multiplier and awarded statutory damages of $432,637 against Mr. Wong. J & J Sports Productions, Inc. v. Miramontes, No. 1:13-cv-01201, Document 19 (E.D. Cal. Sept. 3, 2014). Plaintiff sought $110,000 in statutory damages under 47 U.S.C. 搂 605(e)(3)(C)(i)(II) for unauthorized broadcast interception. The court held the statutory maximum unjustified for a single violation and awarded $10,000 in statutory damages. J & J Sports Productions, Inc. v. Alvarez, No. 1:14-cv-00453, Document 17 (E.D. Cal. Mar. 23, 2015). Joe Hand Promotions, Inc. v. Be, No. 1:15-cv-01164, Document 25 (E.D. Cal. May 4, 2016). Plaintiff sought $170,000 in combined statutory, enhanced, and conversion damages. G & G Closed Circuit Events, LLC v. Nguyen, No. 5:16-cv-04782, Document 25 (N.D. Cal. Mar. 13, 2017). Breach of construction contract. Liquidated damages clause challenged as penalty. Applied Dunlop v New Garage [1915] AC 79 test; clause upheld at HK$1.2M as genuine pre-estimate of loss
Sale of defective machinery. Breach of implied term under Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap 26) s.16. Court assessed actual loss at HK$520K including replacement cost and business interruption
Breach of exclusive distribution agreement. Claimed lost profits over 5 years. Applied remoteness test from Hadley v Baxendale [1854] 9 Exch 341. Appeal reduced award to HK$8.5M as speculative
Commercial lease breach. Liquidated damages clause at HK$500K challenged under Dunlop v New Garage [1915] AC 79 test. Court held HK$350K as genuine pre-estimate; reduced accordingly
Third-party beneficiary claim under Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Ordinance (Cap 623). Beneficiary claimed HK$3.2M for breach of construction warranty. Appeal upheld HK$2.1M award
Indemnity costs awarded in contract dispute. Plaintiff succeeded on substantial issues. Court ordered indemnity basis costs at HK$320K under standard costs taxation principles
Delayed completion of residential development. Liquidated damages HK$8M challenged. Applied Dunlop test; appeal held clause partly penal, reduced to HK$5.5M as genuine pre-estimate
Breach of software development contract. Defective deliverables. Court assessed rectification costs and consequential loss at HK$780K under Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap 26) principles by analogy
Breach of franchise agreement. Claimed lost profits HK$6M. Applied Hadley v Baxendale [1854] 9 Exch 341; appeal affirmed HK$3.8M as foreseeable and not too remote
Breach of supply agreement with liquidated damages clause. Court applied Dunlop v New Garage [1915] AC 79; held HK$900K as genuine pre-estimate of anticipated loss, reduced from HK$1.5M
Breach of consultancy agreement under Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Ordinance (Cap 623). Claimed HK$4.5M for lost project value. Appeal reduced to HK$2.9M as consequential loss too remote
Standard basis costs awarded in complex commercial contract dispute. Taxed at HK$500K. Court noted proportionality under costs rules; initial claim HK$750K reduced on assessment
Construction delay liquidated damages. Dunlop test applied; appeal held clause partly penal. Genuine pre-estimate determined at HK$1.8M; reduction from HK$3M claim sustained
Breach of engineering services contract. Defective design caused additional construction costs. Awarded HK$1.15M under Sale of Goods Ordinance (Cap 26) and common law principles
Breach of joint venture agreement. Claimed lost profits HK$10M. Applied Hadley v Baxendale test; appeal affirmed HK$6.2M as foreseeable loss within first contemplation rule

Medical negligence causing permanent disability. Plaintiff claimed HK$8M general and special damages. Court awarded HK$5.2M applying structured damages approach and Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562 principles
Slip and fall in commercial premises. Occupiers Liability Ordinance (Cap 314) s.2(2) applied. Appeal upheld HK$2.2M for serious leg injury and loss of earning capacity
Defamation by media publication. Defamation Ordinance (Cap 21) applied. Claimed HK$12M for libel and injury to reputation. Appeal reduced to HK$7.5M as excessive; noted chilling effect on free speech
Product liability claim for defective electrical appliance causing fire. Applied Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562. Awarded HK$1.6M for property damage and personal injury

Employee workplace injury under Employees' Compensation Ordinance (Cap 282). Claimed HK$4M for permanent partial disability. Assessed at HK$2.8M including scheduled compensation and common law damages
Professional negligence by auditor. Applied Caparo v Dickman [1990] 2 AC 605 for duty of care. Claimed HK$6M investment losses. Appeal upheld HK$3.9M as foreseeable and proximate
Catastrophic medical negligence causing tetraplegia. Applied Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562. Claimed HK$15M for lifetime care. Appeal increased to HK$9.5M from initial HK$8.7M trial award
Defamation in online forum. Defamation Ordinance (Cap 21) applied. Claimed HK$5M. Court awarded HK$3.4M considering gravity, publication extent, and vindication principles
Occupier's liability for unsafe scaffolding collapse. Occupiers Liability Ordinance (Cap 314) s.2(3) applied. Awarded HK$1.75M for multiple fractures and loss of earnings
Road traffic accident causing traumatic brain injury. Common law negligence applied. Claimed HK$4.5M. Appeal upheld HK$3M after adjustment for contributory negligence at 15%
Solicitor negligence in property transaction. Applied Caparo v Dickman [1990] 2 AC 605. Claimed HK$9M for lost property value. Appeal held HK$5.8M as loss within scope of duty
Employees' Compensation Ordinance (Cap 282) claim for industrial accident. Claimed HK$2M. Assessed at HK$1.35M including statutory schedule and common law top-up for employer negligence
Severe medical negligence causing brain damage to infant. Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562 applied. Claimed HK$12M for lifetime care. Appeal increased from HK$7.2M to HK$7.8M
Structured damages for medical negligence causing permanent disability. Court applied multiplicand-multiplier approach under common law following Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562 principles
Product liability class action for contaminated food products. Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562 applied. Claimed HK$6M for 12 plaintiffs. Appeal allocated HK$4M aggregate damages
Copyright infringement of architectural drawings. Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) s.35 applied. Court assessed damages at HK$1.6M based on lost licensing fees and infringing profit
Trade mark infringement of luxury brand. Account of profits ordered under Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) s.20. Appeal upheld HK$3.2M as infringer's actual profit attributable to mark use
Patent infringement of pharmaceutical process. Patents Ordinance (Cap 514) s.71 applied. Claimed HK$8M lost profits. Appeal reduced to HK$5.1M as non-infringing alternatives available
Copyright infringement with statutory damages election. Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) s.38 allows additional damages. Court awarded HK$950K statutory damages for flagrant infringement
Trade mark infringement damages under Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) s.46. Counterfeit goods case. Court assessed actual damages at HK$2.2M including lost sales and brand dilution
Patent infringement account of profits. Patents Ordinance (Cap 514) s.71. Claimed HK$6M. Appeal held HK$3.9M as proper proportion of total profit attributable to patented feature
Copyright infringement of software code. Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) s.35. Claimed HK$4.5M. Appeal upheld HK$2.8M as reasonable royalty and lost licence fees
Statutory damages for wilful trade mark infringement. Trade Marks Ordinance (Cap 559) s.46. Court awarded HK$1.3M statutory damages reflecting flagrancy and need for deterrence
Patent infringement of electronics design. Patents Ordinance (Cap 514) s.71. Claimed HK$5.5M. Appeal reduced to HK$3.5M after apportionment for non-infringing components
Copyright infringement of film content. Account of profits under Copyright Ordinance (Cap 528) s.38. Court assessed infringer's profit at HK$2.6M from unauthorised distribution revenue
Derivative action for breach of fiduciary duty by directors. Common law derivative action principles. Claimed HK$12M diverted profits. Appeal upheld HK$7.8M as misappropriation proved
Breach of directors' duties under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) s.465 (PENDING_VERIFICATION). Claimed HK$8M for conflicted transaction. Court awarded HK$5.2M as disgorgement of benefit
Unfair prejudice petition under Companies Ordinance (Cap 622) (PENDING_VERIFICATION). Minority shareholder claimed HK$5M for exclusion from management. Appeal ordered buy-out at HK$3.3M
Breach of fiduciary duty and knowing receipt. Claimed HK$15M diverted corporate assets. Common law principles applied. Appeal upheld HK$9.5M as tracing and restitution remedy
Negligent misstatement by directors in investment circular. Claimed HK$6M. Common law duty of care applied. Awarded HK$3.9M as foreseeable reliance damages under Caparo principles
Wrongful dismissal claim under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57) (PENDING_VERIFICATION for s.32). Claimed HK$2.5M for unreasonable dismissal. Appeal upheld HK$1.6M including reinstatement remedy value
Severance payment claim under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57). Statutory formula applied: 2/3 of last monthly wages x reckonable years. Assessed at HK$520K for 18 years service with monthly wage HK$43K

Disability discrimination claim under Disability Discrimination Ordinance (Cap 487). Claimed HK$1.5M for discriminatory termination. Appeal upheld HK$950K including injury to feelings and aggravated damages
Wrongful dismissal of senior executive under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57) (PENDING_VERIFICATION for s.32). Claimed HK$3.5M. Appeal reduced to HK$2.2M as notice period mitigation found
Long service payment claim under Employment Ordinance (Cap 57). Statutory formula: 2/3 of last monthly wages x reckonable years. Assessed at HK$390K for 15 years service with monthly wage HK$39K
依据Dunlop v New Garage [1915] AC 79判定违约金条款构成penalty clause而无效。按Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION损害赔偿范围计算实际损失为HK$2.8M。一审终结。
基于Cap 623第三方权利条例，违约赔偿包括可预见的间接损失。上诉庭维持原判，最终赔偿HK$1.5M；两轮差距约5.3%(<10%)。
工程合同纠纷。一审HK$6.4M，上诉庭改判HK$5.2M。两轮差距18.75%(>10%)，未收敛。引用Hadley v Baxendale原则（PENDING_VERIFICATION）。
房屋租赁合同违约。按Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION计算直接损失与可预见利润损失。一审终结，赔偿HK$720K。
供应合同违约。一审HK$2.55M，上诉维持HK$2.4M。两轮差距约5.9%(<10%)，已收敛。适用Cap 623第三方权利。
违约金条款被认定为惩罚性条款无效。按Dunlop v New Garage标准调整至实际损失HK$1.2M。Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
重大商事合同违约。一审HK$8.4M，终审维持HK$8M。差距约4.8%(<10%)。适用信赖利益原则。
货物买卖合同纠纷。一审HK$1.45M，上诉改判HK$1.1M。差距24.1%(>10%)。Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
服务合同违约。按Cap 623计算直接损失与利润损失。一审终结，HK$2.2M。
联营合同违约。一审HK$3.19M，上诉维持HK$3.1M。差距约2.8%(<10%)。适用Dunlop v New Garage原则。
小型供货合同违约。一审终结，赔偿HK$600K。Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
大型基建合同违约金争议。一审HK$4.0M，上诉维持HK$3.8M。差距5%(<10%)。按Dunlop v New Garage标准调整。
软件开发合同违约。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.75M。引用Cap 623第三方权利条例。
合资协议违约。一审HK$4.1M，上诉改判HK$3.5M。差距14.6%(>10%)。Cap 26 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
咨询合同违约。一审终结，赔偿HK$950K。适用普通法违约赔偿原则。
医疗过失致人身伤害。一审HK$8.8M，上诉维持HK$8.5M。差距3.4%(<10%)。适用Donoghue v Stevenson [1932] AC 562过失原则与Cap 314占用人法律责任。
交通事故人身伤害。一审HK$5.05M，上诉改判HK$4.2M。差距16.8%(>10%)。适用Cap 314占用人法律责任条例。
工业意外人身伤害。一审终结，赔偿HK$3.2M。依据Cap 282雇员补偿条例计算，并叠加普通法赔偿。
严重医疗事故致终身残疾。一审HK$13M，终审维持HK$12.5M。差距3.8%(<10%)。引用Donoghue v Stevenson原则与Cap 314。
滑倒事故人身伤害。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.8M。适用Cap 314占用人法律责任条例第2条谨慎责任。
产品责任致人身伤害。一审HK$6.65M，上诉维持HK$6.5M。差距2.3%(<10%)。适用Donoghue v Stevenson原则。
职业疏忽致财产损失。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.6M。引用Cap 314与Cap 21诽谤条例（PENDING_VERIFICATION）。
建筑事故致重伤。一审HK$8.9M，上诉改判HK$7.8M。差距12.4%(>10%)。适用Cap 314占用人法律责任。
动物袭击致人身伤害。一审终结，赔偿HK$2.4M。适用普通法严格责任原则。
环境污染致健康损害。一审HK$5.4M，上诉维持HK$5.2M。差距3.7%(<10%)。引用Rylands v Fletcher原则（PENDING_VERIFICATION）。
过失致精神损害。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.5M。适用Cap 314占用人法律责任。
重大医疗过失致死。一审HK$11.4M，终审维持HK$11M。差距3.5%(<10%)。适用Donoghue v Stevenson原则与Cap 314。
行人交通事故。一审终结，赔偿HK$1M。适用Cap 314占用人法律责任条例。
诽谤侵权（口头诽谤）。一审HK$4.3M，上诉改判HK$3.6M。差距16.3%(>10%)。适用Cap 21诽谤条例第3条道歉减刑。
过失致财产毁损。一审终结，赔偿HK$2M。适用普通法过失责任原则。
版权侵权（软件复制）。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.8M。依据Cap 528版权条例§35-§38计算损害赔偿。
商标侵权纠纷。一审HK$3.3M，上诉维持HK$3.2M。差距3%(<10%)。Cap 514 §71 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
专利侵权（外观设计）。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.4M。Cap 559 §20 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
商业秘密侵权致利润损失。一审HK$2.48M，上诉维持HK$2.4M。差距3.2%(<10%)。适用Cap 528版权条例相关原则。
假冒商品侵权。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.5M。依据Cap 514商标条例（PENDING_VERIFICATION）与Cap 528版权条例。
网络版权侵权。一审终结，赔偿HK$950K。适用Cap 528 §35版权侵权损害赔偿。
专利侵权（发明）。一审HK$3.25M，上诉改判HK$2.8M。差距13.8%(>10%)。Cap 559 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
小型商标侵权。一审终结，赔偿HK$650K。Cap 514 §71 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
版权侵权致利润损失。一审HK$3.95M，上诉维持HK$3.8M。差距3.8%(<10%)。适用Cap 528 §38计算利润损失。
外观设计侵权。一审终结，赔偿HK$2.1M。Cap 559 PENDING_VERIFICATION。
股东不公平损害诉讼。一审HK$5.75M，上诉维持HK$5.5M。差距4.3%(<10%)。依据Cap 622公司条例第724条。
董事违反信义义务致公司损失。一审HK$3.4M，上诉改判HK$2.8M。差距17.6%(>10%)。适用Cap 622第465条董事勤勉义务。
证券内幕交易民事赔偿。一审终结，赔偿HK$4.2M。依据Cap 571证券及期货条例第281条与第305条。
公司清盘衍生诉讼。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.8M。适用Cap 622公司条例第724条与第732条。
重大上市公司虚假陈述赔偿。一审HK$7.5M，终审维持HK$7.2M。差距4%(<10%)。适用Cap 571第XIVA部内幕消息披露。
非法解雇赔偿。一审终结，赔偿HK$650K。依据Cap 57雇佣条例第70条计算遣散费与额外赔偿。
工伤普通法赔偿叠加。一审HK$1.25M，上诉维持HK$1.2M。差距4%(<10%)。适用Cap 282雇员补偿条例与Cap 57雇佣条例。
歧视性解雇赔偿。一审HK$2.15M，上诉改判HK$1.8M。差距16.3%(>10%)。依据Cap 57雇佣条例与《性别歧视条例》PENDING_VERIFICATION。
未支付最低工资索赔。一审终结，赔偿HK$300K。依据Cap 608最低工资条例每小时$40标准计算欠薪。
长期服务金与遣散费争议。一审终结，赔偿HK$1.6M。依据Cap 57雇佣条例第31条计算遣散费（月薪上限$22,500×2/3×年资）。
Breach of contract for sale of goods. Buyer claimed $500K consequential damages. Court applied Hadley v Baxendale foreseeability limit; reduced to $380K. UCC §2-715

Software development contract breach. Plaintiff claimed lost profits of $850K. Court found damages speculative under Hadley foreseeability; awarded $520K under UCC §2-715(2)(a) after cover mitigation.
Construction delay penalty. Liquidated damages clause upheld at $120K. Court rejected unconscionability challenge under UCC §2-302; found reasonable forecast of actual damages.

Supply chain disruption. Buyer claimed $2.5M consequential damages from supplier breach. Appellate court affirmed trial reduction to $1.75M based on foreseeability under Hadley v Baxendale and UCC §2-715.
Small claims commercial lease dispute. Court awarded $32K attorney fees under prevailing-party fee-shifting clause; reduced from $45K claim as unreasonable hours under UCC-related contract remedy.

Exclusive distribution agreement breach. Trial court awarded $1.35M lost profits. Appellate court reduced to $900K under UCC §2-715; found insufficient evidence for portion of future lost profits. Gap reflects trial-to-appeal reduction.

Service contract for equipment repair. Plaintiff claimed $75K for non-conforming work and subsequent equipment damage. Court awarded $48K under UCC §2-714(1) for value as delivered vs. value as warranted.

Real estate purchase agreement default. Liquidated damages clause set at $500K. Trial court upheld full amount. Appellate court reduced to $350K under UCC §2-302 unconscionability analysis; found penalty element excessive.

Invoice dispute for consulting services. Court awarded $22K in litigation costs including filing fees and deposition costs; reduced from $28K claim for unnecessary expert testimony. Contract remedy under UCC §2-715(1) incidental damages.

Commercial property lease breach by landlord. Plaintiff claimed $4.2M for relocation and lost profits. Appellate court affirmed $2.1M under UCC §2-715 and Restatement (Second) of Contracts §347; found foreseeability satisfied but mitigation required.
Wholesale goods delivery breach. Buyer claimed $150K for non-conforming shipment and resale losses. Court awarded $95K under UCC §2-714; reduced due to failure to mitigate by cover under §2-712.

Franchise agreement termination dispute. Trial court awarded $180K fees. Appellate court affirmed $145K; found $35K excessive for duplicative work under prevailing-party contractual fee-shifting clause.
Vendor non-compete breach liquidated damages. Court upheld $200K as reasonable estimate of harm under UCC §2-302; rejected unconscionability argument based on parties' bargaining positions.

Technology licensing breach. Plaintiff claimed lost profits from excluded market territory. Court applied Hadley foreseeability rule; reduced from $3.6M to $1.8M under UCC §2-715(2)(a). Appellate court affirmed.

Manufacturing equipment supply breach. Plaintiff claimed $600K for defective machinery causing production stoppage. Court awarded $350K under UCC §2-714(3) and §2-715(2)(b); found causation for remaining $250K speculative.

Medical malpractice wrongful death. Estate claimed $2.5M. Appellate court affirmed $1.8M compensatory for lost earnings and emotional distress under Restatement (Second) of Torts §903; found non-economic damages cap inapplicable.

Product liability defective auto. Jury awarded $5M punitive. Appellate court reduced to $1.2M under State Farm v Campbell (538 U.S. 408) ratio guide; found 4:1 ratio constitutional with $300K compensatory. Gap reflects trial-to-appeal reduction.

Commercial truck accident catastrophic injury. Plaintiff claimed $15M for lifetime care and lost earnings. Appellate court affirmed $9.5M under Restatement (Second) of Torts §906; found future medical damages properly supported by expert testimony.

Intentional infliction of emotional distress claim. Plaintiff claimed $800K from employer harassment. Court awarded $350K under Restatement (Second) of Torts §46; found severe emotional distress but insufficient evidence for full claim.

Environmental contamination by industrial defendant. Jury awarded $12M punitive. Appellate court reduced to $3.6M under State Farm v Campbell single-digit ratio guide; applied 3:1 cap with $1.2M compensatory. Gap reflects trial-to-appeal reduction.

Slip and fall premises liability. Plaintiff claimed $450K for hip fracture and rehab. Court awarded $280K under Restatement (Second) of Torts §907; found plaintiff 30% comparatively negligent under state comparative fault statute.

False Claims Act qui tam whistleblower retaliation. Court awarded $150K statutory damages under 31 U.S.C. §3730(h)(1); reduced from $200K claim based on statutory cap for retaliation remedies under federal whistleblower provision.

Pharmaceutical product failure to warn. Jury awarded $8M punitive. Appellate court reduced to $2M under State Farm v Campbell and BMW v Gore (517 U.S. 559); applied 4:1 ratio with $500K compensatory. Affirmed on further review.

Workplace wrongful death from defective industrial equipment. Estate claimed $6M. Appellate court affirmed $4.2M under Restatement (Second) of Torts §903; included $2M economic and $2.2M non-economic damages. Punitive claim denied.

Negligent infliction of emotional distress by healthcare provider. Trial court awarded $1.2M. Appellate court reduced to $500K under Restatement (Second) of Torts §313; found zone of danger rule applied but damages excessive.

Motorcycle collision with municipal vehicle. Plaintiff claimed $3.2M for traumatic brain injury. Court awarded $2.1M under state tort law and Restatement (Second) of Torts §906; reduced for failure to mitigate with helmet use.

Insurance bad faith claim. Trial court awarded $2.5M punitive. Appellate court reduced to $750K under State Farm v Campbell; found 5:1 ratio acceptable with $150K compensatory. Gap reflects trial-to-appeal reduction.

Dog bite and premises liability. Plaintiff claimed $900K for facial scarring and reconstructive surgery. Appellate court affirmed $650K under Restatement (Second) of Torts §509; found owner had knowledge of dangerous propensity.

Anti-discrimination retaliation under 42 U.S.C. §1981a. Court awarded $400K statutory compensatory damages; reduced from $500K claim due to Title VII statutory cap for employer size under 42 U.S.C. §1981a(b)(3).

Aviation wrongful death multi-plaintiff. Estates claimed $18M total. Appellate court affirmed $12.5M under Warsaw Convention limitations and state tort law; apportioned among decedents under Restatement (Second) of Torts §906.

Copyright infringement of photographic works. Plaintiff elected statutory damages under 17 U.S.C. §504(c); court awarded $45K for 3 works ($15K per work) within $30K non-willful statutory range per work.

Music sampling copyright infringement. Plaintiff elected statutory damages under 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1); appellate court affirmed $180K for 6 works at $30K per work (non-willful ceiling). Innocent infringement defense rejected.

Patent infringement pharmaceutical compound. Patentee claimed lost profits of $25M. Court applied Panduit test under 35 U.S.C. §284; reduced to $15M based on market share and non-infringing alternatives. Appellate court affirmed.

Willful copyright infringement of software code. Plaintiff elected statutory damages under 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2); court awarded $300K for 2 works at $150K per work (willful statutory maximum). Defendant challenged willfulness finding.

Trademark counterfeiting and dilution. Plaintiff claimed $8M actual damages. Appellate court affirmed $4.8M under 15 U.S.C. §1117(a); included lost profits and corrective advertising costs. Enhanced damages denied under §1117(b).

Copyright infringement prevailing plaintiff fees. Court awarded $280K attorney fees under 17 U.S.C. §505; reduced from $350K claim for block-billing and excessive paralegal rates. Fee-shifting under Copyright Act discretionary standard.

Copyright infringement of literary work. Plaintiff elected statutory damages under 17 U.S.C. §504(c); court awarded $75K for single work, at mid-range of $750-$30K non-willful statutory scale. Innocent infringement defense partially accepted.

Semiconductor patent reasonable royalty/lost profits hybrid. Plaintiff claimed $120M under 35 U.S.C. §284. Court applied Georgia-Pacific factors; awarded $85M as reasonable royalty plus partial lost profits. Appellate court affirmed apportionment.

Architectural plans copyright infringement. Plaintiff elected statutory damages under 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1); court awarded $225K for 15 works at $15K per work (non-willful). Defendant's innocent use defense rejected.

Trade secret misappropriation (software algorithm). Plaintiff claimed $50M under DTSA and state UTSA. Court awarded $32M based on unjust enrichment and reasonable royalty. Appellate court affirmed damages methodology under 18 U.S.C. §1836(b)(3)(A).

Shareholder derivative action for breach of fiduciary duty. Court awarded $5.6M corporate damages under Delaware DGCL § 220 inspection rights and § 251 merger context; derivative recovery restored to corporation under Zapata v Maldonado doctrine.

Securities fraud class action under Securities Act § 11 and Exchange Act § 10(b). Lead plaintiff claimed $15M. Court awarded $9M based on inflation-in-damages model; reduced for failure to prove loss causation for subset of trades under Dura Pharmaceuticals v Broudo (544 U.S. 336).

Appraisal rights proceeding under Delaware DGCL § 262. Court awarded $1.65M attorney fees from corporation; reduced from $2.2M claim using Delaware's Sugarland factors. Merger fair value previously set at $85 per share.

Corporate fraud against minority shareholders. Trial court awarded $5M punitive. Appellate court reduced to $1.5M under State Farm v Campbell and Delaware corporate standards; found ratio of 3:1 appropriate with $500K compensatory. Gap reflects trial-to-appeal reduction.

Proxy statement fraud under SEC Rule 14a-9. Shareholders claimed $12M. Court awarded $7.2M based on merger price differential; reduced for failure to prove entire fairness standard under Kahn v MFW framework.

FLSA overtime wage violation. Employee claimed $180K unpaid overtime. Court awarded $360K total under 29 U.S.C. §216(b) (double damages); employer failed to prove good faith defense under 29 U.S.C. §260.

Title VII hostile work environment and retaliation. Plaintiff claimed $2.5M. Appellate court affirmed $1.5M under 42 U.S.C. §1981a; included $500K compensatory and $1M punitive within statutory caps for employer size under §1981a(b)(3).

FLSA minimum wage and overtime collective action. Plaintiffs claimed $450K unpaid wages. Court awarded $900K under 29 U.S.C. §216(b) including liquidated damages; appellate court affirmed double damages as employer failed good faith defense under §260.

FLSA prevailing plaintiff attorney fees. Court awarded $300K under 29 U.S.C. §216(b); reduced from $380K claim using lodestar method and Johnson factors. Court found 20% reduction for excessive staffing on routine tasks.

ADA disability discrimination and failure to accommodate. Plaintiff claimed $800K. Appellate court affirmed $520K under 42 U.S.C. §1981a and ADA §102(a); included back pay and front pay with statutory cap adjustment for employer size.
UCC §2-715 allows buyer's consequential damages for breach of sales contract; reduced by court due to failure to mitigate under cover doctrine.
Liquidated damages clause enforced under UCC §2-302 unconscionability review; reduced 30% as penalty-like provision.
Hadley v Baxendale (1854) foreseeability rule applied; appellate court excluded remote lost profits from downstream contracts.
UCC §2-715(2) consequential damages for lost resale profits; reduced on appeal due to insufficient certainty of proof.
Delay damages under construction contract; liquidated amount upheld as reasonable estimate of actual harm at formation.
Hadley foreseeability test applied by Supreme Court; consequential damages limited to losses communicated at contracting.
Small business contract breach; compensatory damages based on cover purchase price differential under UCC §2-712.
Fee-shifting provision enforced; hours reduced by court for block billing and vague time entries.
Breach of supply agreement; UCC §2-715 lost profits reduced on appeal for failure to prove market conditions with reasonable certainty.
Real estate purchase contract breach; compensatory damages measured by difference between contract price and market value.
Penalty clause struck down under UCC §2-302; trial award reduced to actual damages plus interest.
Lost profits claim under Hadley rule; second limb applied as special circumstances were communicated to breaching party.
Service contract breach; compensatory damages for cost of substitute performance under restitution principles.
Supreme Court affirmed foreseeability limitation; consequential damages for lost enterprise value excluded as too remote.
Medical malpractice; compensatory damages for economic loss and pain suffering reduced on appeal for excessive non-economic award.
State Farm v Campbell (2003) ratio guide applied; 145:1 punitive award reduced to 4:1 consistent with due process.
Motor vehicle negligence; compensatory damages include medical expenses lost wages and non-economic harm per state law.
IIED claim; Restatement (Second) of Torts §46 standard applied; damages reduced as conduct deemed reckless not intentional.
Product liability; punitive award remitted under State Farm 9:1 ratio ceiling where compensatory was substantial.
Premises liability slip-and-fall; compensatory damages reduced for comparative negligence at 35% plaintiff fault.
Wrongful death action; economic damages for lost future earnings adjusted by appellate court for discount rate error.
Defamation by corporation; punitive damages reduced under State Farm single-digit ratio after compensatory remittitur.
Toxic tort exposure; compensatory damages for medical monitoring and diminished property value under Restatement (Second) of Torts.
NIED claim; Restatement (Second) of Torts §313 zone-of-danger limitation enforced; damages limited to witnessed events.
Fraudulent misrepresentation; punitive award remitted to 5:1 ratio under State Farm guideposts; reprehensibility moderate.
Catastrophic injury; compensatory damages include lifetime care costs; appellate court affirmed present value calculation.
Minor personal injury; compensatory damages reduced for pre-existing condition under eggshell-skull rule limitation.
Supreme Court vacated punitive award; State Farm requires single-digit ratio when compensatory is already substantial.
Mass tort settlement; compensatory for each plaintiff based on injury severity matrix; convergence after bellwether appeals.
Copyright willful infringement; 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2) statutory damages at $15000 per work for 3 works; court awarded mid-range.
Copyright non-willful infringement; 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1) statutory damages at $7500 per work for 3 works within $750-$30000 range.
Copyright willful infringement of 2 works; 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2) max $150000 per work; reduced on appeal for insufficient willfulness proof.
Patent infringement; 35 U.S.C. §284 lost profits under Panduit test; affirmed after apportionment of non-infringing features.
Trademark counterfeit; 15 U.S.C. §1117 statutory damages elected for 8 counterfeit marks within statutory range.
Copyright exceptional case; 17 U.S.C. §505 fee award; reduced on appeal for partially successful claim.
Pharmaceutical patent; 35 U.S.C. §284 reasonable royalty rejected in favor of lost profits; reduced for market share uncertainty.
Software copyright; 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1) statutory damages at $12000 per work for 3 works; court found non-willful.
Trade secret misappropriation; compensatory damages under DTSA for actual loss and unjust enrichment; reduced for overlap.
Copyright willful infringement; 17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2) $150000 per work for 1 work; reduced as willfulness finding marginal.
Derivative suit for breach of fiduciary duty; compensatory damages for corporate waste; reduced for speculative benefit calculations.
M&A breakup fee; liquidated damages enforced as reasonable forecast of loss under Delaware law; not a penalty.
Securities fraud class action; compensatory damages under SEC Rule 10b-5; reduced for failure to prove loss causation for all trades.
Minority shareholder oppression; compensatory for fair value of shares; reduced on appeal for lack of marketability discount.
Delaware derivative suit fee award; lodestar method applied with 1.5x multiplier for exceptional benefit to corporation.
FLSA §216(b) overtime violation; liquidated damages equal to back pay doubled; employer failed good-faith defense.
Title VII retaliation; 42 U.S.C. §1981a compensatory and punitive capped at $300000 for employer over 500; reduced on appeal.
Sexual harassment; Title VII compensatory for emotional distress; 42 U.S.C. §1981a cap applied at employer size tier.
FLSA minimum wage violation; 29 U.S.C. §216(b) liquidated damages doubling back wages; willful violation found.
Wrongful termination under Section 1981; compensatory and punitive uncapped; reduced on appeal for speculative front-pay award.
UCC §2-715: buyer recovered consequential damages for lost profits from seller's breach; foreseeability per Hadley v. Baxendale (1854) applied.
UCC §2-302: contract clause held unconscionable; damages limited to foreseeable scope under Hadley v. Baxendale foreseeability test.
UCC §2-715(2)(a): consequential damages for lost profits awarded; seller had reason to know buyer's particular requirements.
Hadley v. Baxendale rule applied; damages limited to foreseeable losses arising naturally from breach of supply contract.
UCC §2-714: buyer recovered damages for non-conformity; market-price differential under §2-713 plus incidental damages under §2-715(1).
UCC §2-302: liquidated damages clause struck as unconscionable; court awarded actual compensatory damages instead.
UCC §2-715(2)(b): damages for personal injury from breach of warranty; proximate cause standard applied per Official Comment.
Hadley v. Baxendale second rule: special circumstances communicated to seller; consequential damages awarded for lost resale profits.
UCC §2-712 cover plus §2-715 incidental damages; buyer mitigated by purchasing substitute goods in commercially reasonable manner.
UCC §2-302(1): court found contract terms unconscionable at time of making; equitable adjustment of damages under §2-302(2).
UCC §2-715: lost profits from delayed delivery awarded; general requirements foreseeability standard per Comment 3 to §2-715.
Hadley v. Baxendale limitation applied; damages for breach of construction contract limited to ordinary course of events losses.
UCC §2-714(2): damages measured by difference in value between goods accepted and value if conforming; expert testimony admitted.
UCC §2-715(2)(a): damages for lost volume sales awarded; seller had reason to know buyer was retailer dependent on timely delivery.
UCC §2-302: procedural unconscionability found; contract of adhesion terms voided, damages limited to restitutionary recovery.
Hadley v. Baxendale first rule: damages arising naturally from breach awarded; buyer recovered price differential under UCC §2-713.
UCC §2-715: incidental damages for inspection and transportation of rejected goods; commercially reasonable charges under §2-715(1).
UCC §2-302(1): substantive unconscionability in consumer contract; damages reduced to avoid oppression or surprise to buyer.
Hadley v. Baxendale applied to service contract; consequential damages limited to losses within contemplation of parties at formation.
UCC §2-714(3): damages for breach of warranty in commercial context; revocation of acceptance and recovery of purchase price difference.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908(2): punitive damages awarded for reckless indifference to plaintiff's rights; reprehensibility guidepost from State Farm v. Campbell (2003) applied.
State Farm v. Campbell (2003): punitive damages reduced to 1:1 ratio; $1M compensatory, $25M punitive after trial court remittitur.
BMW v. Gore (1996): $4M punitive on $4,000 compensatory held unconstitutional; guideposts on reprehensibility and ratio applied.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908: compensatory damages for physical injury and emotional distress; foreseeability standard applied.
State Farm v. Campbell: conduct within single-state boundaries; out-of-state evidence of dissimilar conduct excluded from punitive calculation.
BMW v. Gore: reprehensibility low because economic harm only; ratio of 500:1 held grossly excessive under Due Process Clause.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908(c): punitive damages require compensatory foundation; some jurisdictions allow nominal damages basis.
State Farm v. Campbell: single-digit ratio between punitive and compensatory damages typically satisfies due process; 9:1 upper bound.
Liebeck v. McDonald's (N.M. 1994): $2.7M punitive reduced to $480K; compensatory $160K for third-degree burns from hot coffee spill.
Grimshaw v. Ford Motor Co. (1981): $125M punitive for Pinto gas tank design; reduced to $3.5M on appeal; reprehensibility found high.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908(2): wealth of defendant considered in punitive assessment; deterrence rationale emphasized.
State Farm v. Campbell: three guideposts—reprehensibility, ratio to compensatory, comparable civil/criminal penalties—evaluated by court.
BMW v. Gore: no physical harm, only economic loss; reprehensibility low, requiring modest ratio; due process limits on excessive awards.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908: punitive damages may be awarded for malicious, wanton, or reckless conduct; jury discretion bounded.
State Farm v. Campbell: $145M punitive reduced to $25M; Utah Supreme Court had restored full amount; Supreme Court reversed 6:3.
Grimshaw v. Ford: evidence of cost-benefit analysis placing dollar value on human life supported high reprehensibility finding.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908 comment b: punitive damages are punishment; defendant's mental state of evil motive or recklessness required.
Liebeck v. McDonald's: $640K total after remittitur; prior complaints of 700+ burn injuries admitted to show notice and reprehensibility.
State Farm v. Campbell: Campbell's bad-faith refusal to settle within policy limits; $1M emotional distress and $25M punitive upheld.
BMW v. Gore: nondisclosure of repainted car as new; $4,000 actual damages; $4M punitive; Alabama Supreme Court had affirmed 500:1.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908: deterrence function of punitive damages; wealth of defendant relevant to deterrence calculation.
Grimshaw v. Ford: Ford's internal memo on $11 per car fix vs. $49.5M in predicted burn deaths; evidence of conscious disregard.
State Farm v. Campbell: physical harm vs. economic harm distinction; physical harm increases permissible ratio under reprehensibility factor.
Restatement (Second) of Torts §908: award of punitive damages within discretion of trier of fact; judicial review for constitutional excess.
BMW v. Gore: comparable civil penalties for deceptive trade practices in Alabama were $2,000; $4M punitive far exceeded civil penalties.
17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1): statutory damages awarded for copyright infringement; $750 per work minimum to $30,000 per work ceiling.
17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2): willful infringement found; statutory damages enhanced to $150,000 per work; statutory maximum applied.
35 U.S.C. §284: reasonable royalty awarded for patent infringement; damages adequate to compensate but no less than reasonable royalty.
35 U.S.C. §284: lost profits plus reasonable royalty; court trebled damages for willful infringement under §284 enhanced damages provision.
15 U.S.C. §1117(a): trademark infringement; defendant's profits and plaintiff's actual damages awarded; costs of action included.
15 U.S.C. §1117(c)(1): statutory damages for counterfeit mark; $1,000 to $200,000 per counterfeit mark per type of goods.
15 U.S.C. §1117(c)(2): willful counterfeit mark use; statutory damages enhanced to $2,000,000 per mark per type of goods.
17 U.S.C. §505: attorney's fees and costs awarded to prevailing copyright plaintiff; willful infringement supported fee-shifting.
35 U.S.C. §284: design patent infringement; disgorgement of infringer's profits under 35 U.S.C. §289 plus reasonable royalty under §284.
Romag Fasteners v. Fossil (2020): willful infringement not required for disgorgement of profits under 15 U.S.C. §1117; recklessness suffices.
17 U.S.C. §504(b): actual damages and infringer's profits awarded; attributable profits not counted in computing actual damages.
35 U.S.C. §284: expert testimony on reasonable royalty admitted; Georgia-Pacific factors applied to determine hypothetical negotiation.
15 U.S.C. §1117(b): treble damages for willful trademark infringement; profits or damages whichever is greater, plus attorney's fees.
17 U.S.C. §504(c)(1): statutory damages elected by plaintiff in lieu of actual damages; court discretion within $750-$30,000 range per work.
35 U.S.C. §284: entire market value rule rejected; damages limited to patented feature's incremental value over prior art.
15 U.S.C. §1117(d): statutory damages for cybersquatting under §1125(d)(1); $1,000 to $100,000 per domain name as court considers just.
17 U.S.C. §505: prevailing defendant awarded fees only in exceptional cases; willful infringement by plaintiff supported fee award against plaintiff.
35 U.S.C. §284: pre-judgment interest on patent damages awarded from date of infringement; rate set by court under §284.
15 U.S.C. §1114(1): likelihood of confusion found; injunctive relief under §1116 plus damages and profits under §1117(a) awarded.
17 U.S.C. §504(c)(2): willful infringement and bad faith; enhanced statutory damages to $150,000 per work; deterrent purpose emphasized.
Securities Act §11: damages for misrepresentation in registration statement; difference between purchase price and value at suit date.
Exchange Act §10(b) and Rule 10b-5: securities fraud; out-of-pocket damages—difference between purchase price and true value at purchase.
DGCL §262: appraisal rights in merger; fair value exclusive of any element of value arising from merger itself; interest at 5% above Fed rate.
Securities Act §11: due diligence defense rejected; underwriter liable for untrue statements; damages reduced by post-issuance market decline.
Exchange Act §10(b): scienter requirement satisfied; recklessness standard under Tellabs; damages based on inflation amount at purchase.
DGCL §262(k): appraisal petition withdrawn without court approval after 60 days; stockholder received merger consideration instead.
Securities Act §11: proportionate liability for non-fraud violations; each defendant liable for percentage of total damages matching fault.
Exchange Act §10(b): loss causation proven; plaintiff's losses caused by disclosure of fraud, not market trends; damages recoverable.
DGCL §262(g): de minimis exception applied; appraisal rights dismissed as shares entitled to appraisal <1% and value <$1M.
Securities Act §11: statute of limitations—3 years from offering, 1 year from discovery; damages barred for late-filed claims.
Exchange Act §10(b): class action settlement; damages based on artificial inflation per Dura Pharmaceuticals loss causation standard.
DGCL §262: fair value determined by discounted cash flow; Court of Chancery applied expert testimony on future cash flows.
Securities Act §11: forward-looking statements protected by safe harbor; no damages for projections with meaningful cautionary language.
Exchange Act §10(b): disgorgement of short-swing profits under §16(b); statutory liability without proof of actual damages.
DGCL §262: interest on fair value from effective date of merger until payment date; statutory rate of Fed discount rate plus 5%.
FLSA §216(b): unpaid overtime wages plus equal amount as liquidated damages; good faith defense rejected by employer.
Title VII §1981a: compensatory damages for intentional discrimination; emotional pain and suffering; cap at $300,000 for 500+ employees.
ADA Title I, 42 U.S.C. §12117: remedies same as Title VII; back pay and compensatory damages for disability discrimination.
FLSA §216(b): willful violation found; 3-year statute of limitations applied; back pay plus liquidated damages awarded.
Title VII §1981a(b)(3): punitive damages capped per employer size; 201-500 employees cap at $200,000; reprehensibility moderate.
ADA §107: reasonable accommodation failure; back pay and front pay awarded; compensatory damages for emotional distress under §1981a.
FLSA §216(b): collective action certified; 120 employees joined; unpaid overtime computed from time records and testimony.
Title VII §1981a: race discrimination; §1981 (no cap) also available; plaintiff elected §1981a capped remedy with lower evidentiary burden.
FLSA §216(b): Portal-to-Portal Act §7 good faith defense failed; employer lacked reasonable grounds for believing compliance.
ADA §102, 42 U.S.C. §12112: direct threat defense rejected; employer liable for failure to hire; back pay and emotional distress damages.
18 U.S.C. §3571(d): fine twice gross gain or loss; defendant derived $25M from wire fraud; fine set at $50M, restitution under MVRA §3663A.
MVRA 18 U.S.C. §3663A: mandatory restitution for offense against property by fraud; victim losses $12M; full restitution ordered.

MVRA §3663A(b)(1): property loss restitution at greater of value on date of damage or sentencing date; $8.5M restitution to victims.

ERISA §502(a)(1)(B): benefit claim denied; court ordered payment of vested pension benefits plus prejudgment interest.
